title: Fix AdminGuard: Claim Admin Access flow not working after Internet Identity login

requirements.text: Perform a deep audit and full rewrite of the AdminGuard component and the backend claimAdmin / checkAdminStatus flow. The fix must address: (1) After a successful Internet Identity login, if no admin principal has been registered in the backend yet, the authenticated user must see a clearly visible 'Claim Admin Access' button — not an 'Access Denied' message. (2) Clicking that button must call the backend `claimAdmin` function, correctly handle all return variants (success, alreadyClaimed, notAuthenticated, and any __kind__-tagged variants), and on success immediately re-fetch admin status and redirect to /admin dashboard. (3) If an admin is already registered and the current user is NOT that admin, show 'Access Denied' with no claim button. (4) Ensure the component never incorrectly treats a freshly authenticated identity as unauthenticated due to actor caching, stale identity, or race conditions between auth state and actor instantiation. Invalidate and refetch admin-status queries immediately after login completes and after a successful claimAdmin call. (5) Verify the backend `claimAdmin` stores the caller's principal when adminPrincipal is null/None, and that `checkAdminStatus` returns true for that principal on all subsequent calls. (6) The fix must work correctly in both Safari and Chrome browsers.

quotes: still getting same issue and also now I am test on chrome as well but it's not working can you please fix this ?

acceptanceCriteria: After logging in via Internet Identity on a fresh deployment with no admin registered, the user sees a 'Claim Admin Access' button (not just 'Access Denied').
Clicking 'Claim Admin Access' calls the backend claimAdmin function without error.
On a successful claimAdmin response, the admin status is re-fetched and the user is redirected to the /admin dashboard.
If an admin is already registered and the logged-in user is not that admin, 'Access Denied' is shown with no claim button.
The backend checkAdminStatus / isAdmin query returns true for the principal that successfully called claimAdmin.
The backend claimAdmin function stores the caller's principal when no admin has been registered yet (adminPrincipal is null/None).
The fix works correctly in both Chrome and Safari browsers.
No race condition exists between Internet Identity auth state settling and the AdminGuard querying admin status — queries are invalidated and refetched after login.
All claimAdmin return variants (including __kind__-tagged variants) are handled gracefully in the frontend.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts or frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts directly — use safe alternatives or wrapper logic.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor file.
Do not break existing admin CRUD functionality for authenticated admins.
nonGoals: Changing the Internet Identity provider or authentication mechanism.
Adding a second admin role or multi-admin support.
Modifying any public-facing pages or non-admin routes.
